rulings.Case Title: TIM ROBINSON INDIVIDUALLY, AND WHORL LLC, VS. COOLEY GODWARD KRONISH LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2011, Line 3, DEFENDANTS PETER WERNER, KENNETH GUERNSEY, BARRY GRAYNOR, CRAIG JACOBY, COOLEY LLP'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action and overruled as to the first cause of action. Plaintiffs failed to plead an actionable fraudulent misrepresentation, concealment or negligent misrepresentation. The first cause of action is adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TIM ROBINSON INDIVIDUALLY, AND WHORL LLC, VS. COOLEY GODWARD KRONISH LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2011, Line 3, DEFENDANT JOHN ROGERS' DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action and overruled as to the seventh cause of action. Plaintiffs failed to plead an actionable fraudulent misrepresentation, concealment, negligent misrepresentation, or a promise made by this Defendant without the intent to perform. As previously determined, the individual executives cannot be held liable for representations/agreements that were made by Solidus. The seventh cause of action is adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RAMA MODY VS. IGOR KRYAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn Terminating Sanctions; Req For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 12, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFF RAMA MODY's Motion Terminating Sanctions; Req For Judicial Notice. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion for Terminating Sanctions DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Plaintiff's notice of motion does not comply with C.C.P. section 2023.040: type of terminating sanction sought not specified (see C.C.P. section 2023.030(d) et seq.), amount of monetary sanction not specified, declaration setting forth facts supporting the amount of any monetary sanction sought not filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LAUREL TIMMS et al VS. YING Y. WANG AKA THERESA WANG, TRUSTEE OF THE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming That Request For Admissions Be Admitted And Order Compelling Discovery, And Awarding Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 12, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS LAUREL TIMMS, MATT MCNALLY's Motion For Order Deeming That Request For Admissions Be Admitted And Order Compelling Discovery, And Awarding Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Deeming that RFAs be Admitted is DENIED. Pursuant to C.C.P. section 2033.280(c), the Court finds that defendants Y.Y. WANG, A. WANG, and D. WANG have served, before hearing on the motion, responses to Plaintiff's requests for admission that are in substantial compliance with Section 2033.220. Plaintiff's Request for Sanctions is GRANTED. Pursuant to C.C.P. section 2033.280(c), the Court imposes a monetary sanction in the amount of $1,010.25 on defendants Y.Y. WANG, A. WANG, and D. WANG jointly and severally, whose failure to serve timely responses to plaintiff's requests for admission necessitated the instant motion. Plaintiff shall prepare an Order for the Court's signature at hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LAUREL TIMMS et al VS. YING Y. WANG AKA THERESA WANG, TRUSTEE OF THE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Resposnes And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 12, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS LAUREL TIMMS, MATT MCNALLY's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Further Discovery is DENIED. Plaintiff's Request for Sanctions is DENIED. Pursuant to C.C.P. section 2030.300(d), the Court finds that on July 9, 2012, defendants Y.Y. WANG, A. WANG, and D. WANG served verified amended discovery responses that make the imposition of the requested sanction unjust. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SANTANDER CONSUMER USA INC. AS ASSIGNEE OF GEMB VS. JOE FAZIO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of And Mtn To Deem Facts As Admitted; Memo Of P & A; Dec; Req. For Attorney Fees Of $200.00 Plus Filing Fee Of $40.00 And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 12, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFF SANTANDER CONSUMER USA INC. AS ASSIGNEE OF GEMB's Motion To Deem Facts As Admitted; Request For Attorney Fees Of $200.00 Plus Filing Fee Of $40.00. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2011, Line 14, DEFENDANT ANDREAS PAPALIOLIOS' Special MOTIOn To Strike By DefENDANt Andreas G. Papaliolios. Continued on the court's own motion to July 17, 2012. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NADER SHABAHANNGI VS. 124 HAIGHT STREET, LLC, CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Appointment Of A Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2011, Line 15, PLAINTIFF NADER SHABAHANNGI'S MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF A RECEIVER. Denied. Plaintiff has not shown that the appointment of a receiver is either necessary or appropriate under Code of Civil Procedure section 564. Based on Defendant's representation in his opposition papers that he intends to continue to apply all rent payments received from 124-126 Haight Street to the payment of real property taxes and mortgages on the properties, he is required to do so pending final resolution of this case or other court order to the contrary. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VISITACTION VALLEY COMMUNITY CENTER, A CALIFORNIA VS. MIESHA BELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Relief From Labor Code Section 98.2 (B)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2011, Line 16, APPELLANT VISITACTION VALLEY COMMUNITY CENTER'S Motion For Relief From Labor Code Section 98.2 (B). Denied. The moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers as required by Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
